trade.summary: India exports pharmaceuticals, engineering goods, chemicals, textiles, and IT services to Sudafrica. Sudafrica exports precious metals and minerals (gold, platinum, chromium), coal, and vehicles. India runs a trade surplus. Sudafrica is India's largest trading partner in Africa. The India-Southern African Customs Union (SACU) trade relationship is growing.

diplomatic.summary: India and Sudafrica have a 'Strategic Partnership' with deep historical ties. Both nations were connected through Gandhi's experiences in Sudafrica, which shaped his philosophy. Both are founding IBSA members (India-Brasile-Sudafrica) and BRICS members. PM Modi and Sudafrican presidents have met at multiple multilateral forums. Sudafrica is India's gateway to the African continent.

military.summary: Growing defence cooperation. India has provided defence equipment (BrahMos missile interest, corvettes). Joint naval exercises (IBSAMAR) as part of the IBSA trilateral framework. India-Sudafrica defence committee meets regularly.

financial.summary: Indian companies (Mahindra, Tata, Cipla, Dr Reddy's) have significant presence in Sudafrica. Sudafrica's mining sector attracts Indian investment. Indian pharmaceutical generics are critical for Sudafrica's healthcare system. Indian IT companies serve Sudafrican corporate clients.

energy.summary: Sudafrica imports Indian refined petroleum products. India imports some Sudafrican coal. Cooperation on renewable energy transitions is growing. India and Sudafrica co-lead some BASIC (Brasile-Sudafrica-India-Cina) climate negotiations.

scientific.summary: Scientific cooperation through IBSA, BRICS, and bilateral agreements. Joint research in health (HIV/AIDS, TB), space science (ISRO-SANSA cooperation), and astronomy (SALT telescope). Sudafrican universities host Indian academic collaborations. SKA (Square Kilometre Array) radio telescope in Sudafrica involves Indian scientific contributions.
